notes:
làm vậy = do that / do it like that. It refers to an action that was mentioned before or is obvious from context.

It’s làm (do) + vậy (like that), used to point back to someone’s action. You use it when reacting, questioning, or commenting on what someone did.

Very close to làm như vậy (slightly more explicit). Opposite idea: làm thế này = do it like this (near / showing how).
exampleSentences: Anh không hiểu tại sao em lại làm vậy trong tình huống đó.
Nếu chị làm vậy, mọi người sẽ cảm thấy ngạc nhiên đấy.
Em nghĩ không nên làm vậy khi chưa hỏi ý kiến bố mẹ.
exampleSentencesTranslated: I don't understand why you would do that in that situation.
If you do that, everyone will be surprised.
I think you shouldn't do that without asking your parents.
notes_md:
**làm vậy** = *do that / do it like that*. It refers to an action that was mentioned before or is obvious from context.

It’s **làm** (do) + **vậy** (like that), used to point back to someone’s action. You use it when reacting, questioning, or commenting on what someone did.


Very close to **làm như vậy** (slightly more explicit). Opposite idea: **làm thế này** = do it like this (near / showing how).
